body.title: audit-expert weekly 2026-07-10
body.text:
## Expert audit — week ending 2026-07-10 (weekly, 3 day-seeded experts)

Context: research project, single-host dev rack, no production (per standing guidance).
Reliability/Security lenses read against the DEV rack, not a prod deployment.

### Product Manager — "delivering value toward the factory goal?"
- STRONG week for product, not just infra: the INTENTION->DESIGN->TICKET compiler stack
  shipped into the skills (design first-class, /intend front boundary, re-key). This IS the
  factory-of-factories thesis made concrete, not scope creep.
- ROOT-CAUSE finding: the outcome loop had NEVER closed (0 outcomes ever recorded) because
  /outcome wrote to decisions/ while /weekly-retro read designs/-first. "Measure don't declare"
  was structurally impossible until today's fix (@193653aa). Capability claims this week rest on
  proofs (good) but have zero outcome-validation history — that starts next week.
- BLOCKED: full-suite verification + capability eval both gated on Hex (single inference host,
  down tonight). LLM-middle deliberately deferred (correct scoping).

### Reliability Engineer — "operational story when things break?"
- SINGLE POINT OF FAILURE: Hex (10.0.0.100) is the only live inference source. Down => 314 tests
  fail, /eval-run cannot run, DS/aider builders can't build. No live fallback (OpenRouter stripped
  for cost — intentional, but it means zero inference redundancy). Known constraint
  (DS-off-until-free-inference), re-flagged as the dominant fragility.
- 124 SILENT_EXCEPT handlers swallow errors => longer diagnosis when a device breaks. Concentrated
  in third-party (ebook_drm) but present in live devices (web_server, inference, granny, postgres).
  These are gaps in the "log every interface crossing" (AR-009) reconstruct-incident guarantee.
- POSITIVE: the dev-process itself now has strong operational observability — path-moves monitor,
  consequence-aging, validity-sweep, gate-sweep all ran clean/near-clean in this day-close.

### Security Engineer — "adversarial inputs / trust boundaries?"
- Creds clean: only hit is a third-party Kindle-DRM constant; no live keys in source (.env gitignored).
- HARDENED this week: OpenRouter-var strip moved into rescueclaude (the single launch chokepoint),
  closing the 401/credential-leak regression (incident_superclaude_401). Good trust-boundary work.
- STANDING GAP (low risk on single-host research rack, named each pass): PgBus envelopes carry
  from/to_device but there's no evident channel auth / replay-protection; the audit trail's
  completeness is undercut by the 124 silent-excepts. Acceptable now; would need attention before
  any multi-tenant / networked deployment.

### Net
No new tickets warranted — findings are either shipped-this-week wins, known/intentional
constraints (Hex SPOF, LLM-middle deferral), or pre-existing debt already tracked
(silent-excepts overlap the IMAP purge + general hygiene). The one genuinely actionable
item is next week's: START closing the outcome loop now that it mechanically can.
